message:
fix(indexer): plumb flow_limiter release + harden flaky-test env isolation

Two concurrent changes to src/indexer/mod.rs from the ongoing rebuild-
pipeline hardening:

1. Flow-limiter byte-accounting plumb-through
   spawn_lexical_rebuild_shard_builder_workers now receives an
   Arc<StreamingByteLimiter>. Each worker sums flow_reservation_bytes
   across the packets it consumed and calls flow_limiter.release() after
   the shard index is built. This closes a leak where reserved bytes
   never got returned to the budget when shards built successfully —
   downstream producers could stall against a limiter that had no live
   reservations but also no free capacity. The rebuild_tantivy_from_db_
   via_staged_shards call site was updated to clone the limiter into
   the worker spawn.

2. Test serialization + env guards
   Added #[serial] and CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_DISABLE=1 scoped env guards
   to four tests that were racing with each other or with the
   responsiveness governor when cargo-nextest ran them concurrently:
   - wal_autocheckpoint_defaults_follow_bulk_import_mode
   - lexical_rebuild_pipeline_settings_snapshot_honors_env_overrides
   - lexical_rebuild_packet_producer_preserves_order_across_parallel_page_prep_workers
   - streaming_consumer_handles_mixed_startup_batches_with_watch_checkpoint_policy
   - rebuild_tantivy_from_db_promotes_pipeline_budgets_after_first_commit
   - initial_batch_fetch_limit_defaults_to_bounded_warmup_chunk

No production-code env reads changed; only the test scaffolding.
